Hey, welcome back again. Listen, I'm really excited for this module right now because if there's one thing in my life I'm about, it's how to get the best result in the shortest possible period of time. Listen, I hate wasting my own time and I'm sure you hate wasting yours as well. What you're going to discover in this module is how to make sure that you're spending your time on the people who matter and who both want and need your product or service. This is about prospecting, the science of targeting your ideal buyers. You've probably heard me say the straight line isn't about closing everybody because there are some people who simply aren't closeable and others who simply shouldn't be closed. What this is about is making sure you're talking to the right people and then getting those people who are on the fence, the ones who are reserved only for the very best sales people up until now, get those people to say yes and it all starts with prospecting. So before I begin, I want to debunk a couple of myths here. Myth number one is that you qualify and then you're done, meaning you only qualify in the beginning. The fact of the matter is you're always qualifying. You're constantly asking questions, constantly gathering intelligence and working to uncover the important stuff that you need to know to close the sale, not the smoke screens that you typically get in the beginning when someone first opens up their mouth. Now the other myth is that prospecting is only about qualifying in terms of whether or not they need your product or they have money and so on. This is only a very small part of the equation. The bigger part of the equation is identifying not only your prospect's needs but also their beliefs, their values, their past experiences with other sales people and of course pinpointing their pain. And you're doing all of that while you're getting into this ultra tight rapport with them on both a conscious and an unconscious level and of course using tonality to do that. See what I mean by this is that if you're doing a good job when you're done qualifying, so when you've been doing two things. Number one, you've built massive rapport with them on both a conscious and an unconscious level and two, you've completely exhaustively gathered as much intelligence as you need, which includes number one, identifying their needs, two, uncovering exactly where their pain is and of course amplifying it if necessary and then all the other stuff we talked about, beliefs, values and everything else that you might ever need to know to really get to that point where you can close the sale. Now remember if you're just focused on money, like can they afford my product, you've done yourself a huge disservice. This is simply just one part of the sale to ask about money if they qualify. Prospecting is just not something that you rush, it's something you take your time with and in fact if you're truly an expert in your field, you're not only entitled to ask questions but you're basically obligated to find out what someone's needs are, where their pain is. I mean how can you sell someone something if you really don't know what their exact needs are and where their exact pain lies? That's the higher level of qualifying and you need to do all of that and build massive rapport along the way so you can qualify someone and be the interrogator, which is what a lot of people do and it's wrong, or you can qualify someone from the position of the trusted advisor, the friend. Everyone who's there to tailor a product, an idea to help them get what they want to directly fill their needs and to suit them pretty much in every way. And if you do your job here and the prospect truly feels that you understand them, that you care about their needs and that your desire to closing is just secondary, then the fact is that closing becomes a hell of a lot easier.
